ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE INC'S MOTION TO TRANSFER. MATTER ARGUED AND SUBMITTED. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED. DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE INC.'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS DENIED ON THE PROPER COURT GROUND BECAUSE IT IS UNTIMELY. DEFENDANT FILED AN ANSWER AND DID NOT FILE A MOTION TO TRANSFER AT THAT TIME. (C.C.P. 396B(A), 397(A).) EVEN IF THE COURT COULD CONSIDER A DISCRETIONARY TRANSFER PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 397(A), DEFENDANT FAILED TO MEET ITS BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT SAN FRANCISCO IS NOT THE PROPER COURT. FINALLY, EVEN ASSUMING SAN FRANCISCO IS NOT THE PROPER COURT, DEFENDANT MAY ONLY MOVE TO CHANGE VENUE TO ITS RESIDENCE AND DEFENDANT DID NOT SHOW THAT ITS RESIDENCE IS IN SACRAMENTO COUNTY. AS TO C.C.P. 397(C), DEFENDANT FAILED TO MEET ITS BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT THE CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES AND THE ENDS OF JUSTICE WILL BE PROMOTED BY TRANSFER. ORDER SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE GARRETT WONG, CLERK M. JONES, EARLY K. LANGLEY, CSR 3537 (503)

BEFORE A PARTY FILES AND SERVES SUCH MOTION, IT MUST CONTACT THE CALENDAR CLERK TO MAKE A RESERVATION. ELECTRONIC SERVICE IS CONSIDERED THE EQUIVALENT OF PERSONAL SERVICE. ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON PLAINTIFFS' CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN UNDER EITHER PRONG OF AGUILAR V. ATLANTIC RICHFIELD CO. (2001) 25 CAL.4TH 826. DEFENDANT FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFFS DO NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT ACTED WITH THE REQUISITE MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION TO WARRANT THE IMPOSITION OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFFS' RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES PRECLUDE DEFENDANT FROM SUSTAINING ITS BURDEN UNDER THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. DEFENDANT ALSO FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF PRESENTING EVIDENCE THAT CONCLUSIVELY NEGATES AN ELEMENT OF PLAINTIFFS' PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM. EVEN IF THE COURT CONSIDERS ALL OF DEFENDANT'S EVIDENCE TO WHICH PLAINTIFFS OBJECT, IT IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO SUSTAIN DEFENDANT'S BURDEN. DEFENDANT FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PUBLIC POLICY DICTATES THAT ITS MOTION SHOULD BE GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: GARRETT L. The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, RULES that the following are entitled to distribution of all net funds from the survival/wrongful death lawsuit and claims against asbestos bankrupt defendant trusts negotiated after the July 11, 2017 death of Danny Shaw in the following proportions as follows:

Estate of Danny Shaw - 0%.
Shirley Shaw - 25%.
Brenda Shaw - 25%.
Bradley Shaw - 25%.
Byron Shaw - 25%.
Danny Shaw, Jr. - 0%.
David Shaw - 0%.

All future net funds derived from settlements entered into after the July 11, 2017 death of Danny Shaw shall be distributed in the percentages set forth on page 2, paragraph 1 of the Order approving the motion for apportionment.

The signature of plaintiff Shirley Shaw alone is sufficient to bind the estate and wrongful death heirs on all settlement documents.

Proposed Order to Apportion and Distribute Funds was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On Plaintiffs Claim For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Oct-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Tiffany Viveros of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Danny Shaw and Shirley Shaw

RULING:
DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON PLAINTIFFS' CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from sustaining its burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence that conclusively negates an element of plaintiffs' punitive damages claim. Even if the Court considers all of defendant's evidence to which plaintiffs object, it is not sufficient to sustain defendant's burden. Defendant failed to demonstrate that public policy dictates that its motion should be granted. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE INC. MOTION TO TRANSFER

Appearances: Kimberly Chu of Brayton Purcell(415.898.1555)for Plaintiff Danny Shaw, et al.

Evanthia Spanos Designated Defense Counsel of Spanos Przetak (510.250.0200), Alyssa L. Cohan of Walsworth WFBM, LLP (415.781.7072)for Defendant Thomas Dee Engineering, Justin L. Clark of Selman Breitman, LLP (415.979.0400) for Lamos Gasket Co.& Fireman's Fund Insurance Co. (Intervenors) Natalie Garcia Lashinsky of Pond North, LLP (415.217.1240) for Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc., Daniel D. O'Shea of Perkins Cole (415.344.7002 for Defendant Honeywell, Julie A. Torres of DeHay Elliston, LLP (510.285.0752) for Defendant Kaiser Gypsum, Natalie Lashinsky of Pond North, LLP for Defendant Faser's Boiler Service.

Matter Argued and submitted. The tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion to transfer venue to Sacramento County is denied. The motion is denied on the proper court ground because it is untimely. Defendant filed an answer and did not file a motion to transfer at that time. (C.C.P. 396b(a), 397(a).) Even if the Court could consider a discretionary transfer pursuant to C.C.P. 397(a), defendant failed to meet its burden of demonstrating that San Francisco is not the proper court. Finally, even assuming San Francisco is not the proper court, defendant may only move to change venue to its residence and defendant did not show that its residence is in Sacramento County. As to C.C.P. 397(c), defendant failed to meet its burden of demonstrating that the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice will be promoted by transfer. Order signed and filed in open court. Judge Garrett Wong, Clerk M. Jones, Early K. Langley, CSR 3537 (503)
